formal_definition: biomarker testing is A laboratory method that uses a sample of tissue, blood, or other body fluid to check for certain genes, proteins, or other molecules that may be a sign of a disease or condition, such as cancer. Biomarker testing can also be used to check for certain changes in a gene or chromosome that may increase a person’s risk of developing cancer or other diseases. Biomarker testing may be done with other procedures, such as biopsies, to help diagnose some types of cancer. It may also be used to help plan treatment, find out how well treatment is working, make a prognosis, or predict whether cancer will come back or spread to other parts of the body. Also called molecular profiling and molecular testing.
simplified_explanation:
Okay, I've got it! Here's my attempt to explain biomarker testing in simple language:

Biomarker testing is like a special kind of detective work for doctors. They take a tiny bit of your body fluid, like blood or a piece of tissue, and look for specific clues that could tell them if you have a disease or not. These clues are like fingerprints for your body - they can show if something is wrong and where it might be coming from. This test can help doctors figure out how well treatment is working, see if the cancer might come back, or even predict if you're at risk for getting sick again. It's like having a secret code that only the doctor can read, and it helps them figure out what's going on inside your body.</s>
easy_definition: A lab test of any molecule in your body that can be measured to assess your health. Also called molecular testing.

formal_definition: biopsy is The removal of cells or tissues for examination by a pathologist. The pathologist may study the tissue under a microscope or perform other tests on the cells or tissue. There are many different types of biopsy procedures. The most common types include: (1) incisional biopsy, in which only a sample of tissue is removed; (2) excisional biopsy, in which an entire lump or suspicious area is removed; and (3) needle biopsy, in which a sample of tissue or fluid is removed with a needle. When a wide needle is used, the procedure is called a core biopsy. When a thin needle is used, the procedure is called a fine-needle aspiration biopsy.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's my attempt to explain the term "biopsy" in simple language:

A biopsy is like taking a small piece of evidence from your body to help doctors figure out if there might be something wrong with you. They use a special tool to take out just the right amount of tissue or fluid, and then they look at it under a microscope to see what's going on. There are different kinds of biopsies, like taking just a tiny bit of tissue or removing a whole lump. It's kind of like when you take a small bite out of a cake to see if it tastes good - doctors take a small bite out of your body to see how it's doing.</s>
easy_definition: A procedure that removes fluid or tissue samples to be tested for a disease.

formal_definition: chromosome is Part of a cell that contains genetic information. Except for sperm and eggs, all human cells contain 46 chromosomes.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's my explanation of the term "chromosome" for a non-medical person:

A chromosome is like a recipe book inside a cell. It contains all the instructions for how the cell should grow, develop, and work properly. Just like a recipe book has different recipes for different dishes, a chromosome has different genes that tell the cell how to do different things. And just like you need all the recipes in a recipe book to make a delicious meal, a cell needs all its chromosomes to function correctly. In humans, most cells have 46 chromosomes, except for sperm and egg cells which only have 46 or 47 chromosomes. So, chromosomes are super important for keeping our cells healthy and working properly!</s>
easy_definition: Part of a cell that contains genetic information. Except for sperm and eggs, all human cells contain 46 chromosomes.

formal_definition: clinical trial is A type of research study that tests how well new medical approaches work in people. These studies test new methods of screening, prevention, diagnosis, or treatment of a disease. Also called clinical study.
simplified_explanation:
Certainly! Here's my attempt to explain the term "clinical trial" in simple language for a non-medical person:

A clinical trial is like a test drive for new medical treatments. Imagine you're trying out a new car before buying it - you want to see how it performs on the road, right? In the same way, doctors run clinical trials to see how well a new medicine or procedure works in real people, like you or me. They compare the results of the trial to existing treatments to see if the new one is better. Just as you might try different cars before choosing the one you like best, doctors test different treatments in clinical trials to find the best one for patients.</s>
easy_definition: A type of research that assesses health tests or treatments.

formal_definition: fertility is The ability to produce children.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's my explanation of the term "fertility" in simple language that a non-medical person could understand:

Fertility refers to the ability of a person or couple to have children. It's like planting seeds in soil - if the soil is good quality and there's enough water and sunlight, the seeds will grow into plants. In the same way, if a person or couple has healthy reproductive organs and the right conditions, they can create a new life. Fertility can be affected by many factors, such as age, overall health, and lifestyle choices. For example, some medications or medical conditions can affect fertility, just like how too much rain or not enough sunlight can prevent plants from growing. If someone is having trouble getting pregnant, they may need to see a doctor to figure out what's causing the problem and find a solution.</s>
easy_definition: The ability to produce children.

formal_definition: fertility preservation is A type of procedure used to help keep a person’s ability to have children. A fertility preservation procedure is done before a medical treatment that may cause infertility, such as radiation therapy or chemotherapy. Examples of fertility preservation procedures include sperm banking, egg freezing, in vitro fertilization with embryo freezing, and certain types of surgery for cervical and ovarian cancer.
simplified_explanation: Fertility preservation is like saving seeds for planting a garden. When someone is about to go through medical treatment that might make them unable to have kids later on, they can do a procedure to save their eggs or sperm so they can use them later. It's like putting away some extra seeds in case they want to grow a beautiful flower or vegetable garden in the future. The medical treatments can be like a storm that could ruin the garden, but with fertility preservation, they can protect their chances of growing a family. Some common ways to do this are by freezing eggs or sperm, or by doing special surgeries to keep the reproductive organs safe from the medical treatment.</s>
easy_definition: A way to preserve or protect the ability to become pregnant and have a baby.

formal_definition: gene is The functional and physical unit of heredity passed from parent to offspring. Genes are pieces of DNA, and most genes contain the information for making a specific protein.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's my attempt to explain the term "gene" in a way that's easy to understand for a non-medical person:

A gene is like a recipe book for your body. It contains instructions on how to make different proteins that help your body work properly. Just like a recipe book has recipes for different dishes, a gene has instructions for creating different proteins that do different jobs in your body. These proteins can help you grow, repair yourself, and fight off sicknesses. And just like how you need multiple recipes to make a delicious meal, your body needs multiple genes to make all the proteins it needs to stay healthy.

For example, some genes might give you blue eyes, while others might help you digest food. Some genes might even help protect you against certain diseases. So, your genes are like a set of instructions that help your body function properly and keep you healthy.</s>
easy_definition: The functional and physical unit of heredity passed from parent to offspring. Genes are pieces of DNA, and most genes contain the information for making a specific protein.

formal_definition: gynecologic oncologist is A doctor who has special training in diagnosing and treating cancers of the female reproductive organs.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's my attempt to explain the term "gynecologic oncologist" in a way that's easy to understand for a non-medical person:

A gynecologic oncologist is like a superhero for women's health. They are doctors who have special training to find and treat cancers of the female reproductive system, like ovarian cancer or uterine cancer. Imagine having a magic wand that can only fight bad guys inside your own body - that's what a gynecologic oncologist does! They use their knowledge and skills to help save lives and keep women healthy. Just like how you want a trustworthy friend by your side when you need help, a gynecologic oncologist is there to protect and care for you and your reproductive system.</s>
easy_definition: A doctor who’s an expert in reproductive cancers of those assigned female at birth.

formal_definition: hormone is One of many substances made by glands in the body. Hormones circulate in the bloodstream and control the actions of certain cells or organs. Some hormones can also be made in the laboratory.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's an explanation of the term "hormone" that's easy to understand and free of technical jargon:

A hormone is like a special messenger inside your body. It travels through your bloodstream and goes to different parts of your body, like a mailman delivering letters. When it gets there, it tells the cells or organs what to do, kind of like how you might give your friend instructions on how to get to a certain place. For example, if you have too much sugar in your blood, your pancreas might send out a hormone called insulin to tell your cells to take in some of that extra sugar so it doesn't build up and cause problems. Or, if you're feeling stressed, your adrenal glands might send out a hormone called cortisol to help you feel more calm and relaxed. Making hormones in a lab is like making copies of a letter - you start with one original letter, and then you make lots of copies of it to send out to different places.</s>
easy_definition: A chemical in the body that triggers a response from cells or organs.

formal_definition: medical oncologist is A doctor who has special training in diagnosing and treating cancer in adults using chemotherapy, hormonal therapy, biological therapy, and targeted therapy. A medical oncologist often is the main health care provider for someone who has cancer. A medical oncologist also gives supportive care and may coordinate treatment given by other specialists.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's my attempt to explain the term "medical oncologist" in simple language:

A medical oncologist is a doctor who specializes in treating cancer. They have extra training beyond being a regular doctor to help people with cancer. They use different tools like chemotherapy, hormone therapy, or targeted therapy to try to kill the bad cells that are causing the cancer. They are usually the main doctor taking care of someone with cancer and work with other doctors to give the best care possible. They also try to make sure patients feel comfortable and don't have too many side effects from their treatments. It's like having a superhero for your body's cancer battle!</s>
easy_definition: A doctor who treats cancer in adults using systemic therapy.

formal_definition: menopause is The time of life when a woman’s ovaries stop producing hormones and menstrual periods stop. Natural menopause usually occurs around age 50. A woman is said to be in menopause when she hasn’t had a period for 12 months in a row. Symptoms of menopause include hot flashes, mood swings, night sweats, vaginal dryness, trouble concentrating, and infertility.
simplified_explanation: Menopause is like a natural shutdown of a woman's reproductive system. It happens when her ovaries stop making eggs and hormones that help regulate her monthly period. This usually happens around age 50, but can happen earlier or later depending on each woman. During menopause, women may experience hot flashes, mood changes, and other symptoms. These can be uncomfortable, but they're a sign that the body is making the transition to this new stage of life. Women who have gone through menopause can no longer get pregnant, but many other aspects of their health can improve, like lowered risk of certain diseases.</s>
easy_definition: The end of menstrual periods.

formal_definition: mutation is Any change in the DNA sequence of a cell. Mutations may be caused by mistakes during cell division, or they may be caused by exposure to DNA-damaging agents in the environment. Mutations can be harmful, beneficial, or have no effect. If they occur in cells that make eggs or sperm, they can be inherited; if mutations occur in other types of cells, they are not inherited. Certain mutations may lead to cancer or other diseases. A mutation is sometimes called a variant.
simplified_explanation:
Okay! Here's my attempt to explain the term "mutation" in a way that's easy to understand for a non-medical person:

A mutation is like a mistake in the instructions for making a Lego castle. Imagine you have a blueprint for building a castle, and you're following the steps to build it. But, instead of using the correct pieces, you accidentally grab the wrong ones and try to put them together. The result might look something like a pile of random Legos on the floor. That's kind of like what happens when a cell makes a mutation - it's like a mistake in the instructions for building the cell's own blueprint. And just like with Legos, some mutations can be harmless, while others can cause problems or even lead to disease.</s>
easy_definition: Any change in the DNA sequence of a cell. Mutations may be caused by mistakes during cell division, or they may be caused by exposure to DNA-damaging agents in the environment. Mutations can be harmful, beneficial, or have no effect. If they occur in cells that make eggs or sperm, they can be inherited; if mutations occur in other types of cells, they are not inherited. Certain mutations may lead to cancer or other diseases. A mutation is sometimes called a variant.

formal_definition: oncologist is A doctor who has special training in diagnosing and treating cancer. Some oncologists specialize in a particular type of cancer treatment. For example, a radiation oncologist specializes in treating cancer with radiation.
simplified_explanation: Oncologists are doctors who specialize in treating cancer. They have extra training and knowledge to help them figure out how to stop cancer from growing or spreading. Just like how you might have a favorite teacher for math or English, an oncologist is like a superhero for your body's cancer problems. They use different tools and techniques to fight the cancer cells, such as surgery, medicine, or radiation. Just like how you need to go to a car mechanic to fix your car, you go to an oncologist to fix your body!</s>
easy_definition: A doctor who has special training in diagnosing and treating cancer. Some oncologists specialize in a particular type of cancer treatment. For example, a radiation oncologist specializes in treating cancer with radiation.

formal_definition: ovarian suppression is Treatment that stops or lowers the amount of estrogen made by the ovaries. Types of ovarian suppression include surgery to remove both ovaries, radiation therapy, and the use of certain drugs. Ovarian suppression may be used to prevent and treat breast cancer. Also called ovarian ablation.
simplified_explanation: Ovarian suppression is like putting a pause button on your body's ability to produce eggs. It's a treatment used to stop or reduce the amount of estrogen made by the ovaries. Imagine you have a box of chocolates, and inside each one is an egg. Ovarian suppression is like taking away the box so the eggs can't grow or hatch. This can help prevent or treat certain types of cancer, like breast cancer. There are different ways to do ovarian suppression, like removing both ovaries, using radiation therapy, or taking special medicines. Just like how you might need to take medicine to feel better when you're sick, ovarian suppression can help keep your body healthy from cancer.</s>
easy_definition: Methods used to lower hormone levels made by the ovaries.

formal_definition: ovary is One of a pair of female glands in which the eggs form and the female hormones estrogen and progesterone are made. These hormones play an important role in female traits, such as breast development, body shape, and body hair. They are also involved in the menstrual cycle, fertility, and pregnancy. There is one ovary on each side of the uterus.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's my attempt at explaining the term "ovary" in a way that's easy to understand for a non-medical person:

Hey there! So, did you know that inside your tummy, there are two special gadgets called ovaries? They're like tiny factories where magic happens! Inside these gadgets, little eggs grow and get ready to travel through your body to meet their forever home - your uterus. These eggs are super important because they help create the hormones estrogen and progesterone, which do lots of cool things like help your boobs grow, shape your body, and make your periods happen. Plus, they play a big role in getting you pregnant! So, your ovaries are kind of like tiny wizards inside your tummy, making all sorts of magic happen!</s>
easy_definition: One of a pair of glands that produce eggs and hormones. There is one ovary on each side of the uterus.

formal_definition: pathologist is A doctor who has special training in identifying diseases by studying cells and tissues under a microscope.
simplified_explanation:
Okay, here's my attempt at explaining the term "pathologist" to a non-medical person in simple language:

A pathologist is like a detective for doctors. They look at tiny details of cells and tissues under a microscope to figure out what's making someone sick. Imagine you have a mystery box full of tiny pieces of evidence, like fibers or blood cells. A pathologist would examine each piece closely to figure out what it is and how it could help solve the mystery of why someone is feeling unwell. Just like a detective needs to use all their clues to solve a crime, a pathologist uses their knowledge and skills to diagnose diseases based on these tiny details. It's a really important job because it helps doctors know how to treat people properly and make them feel better!</s>
easy_definition: A doctor who has special training in identifying diseases by studying cells and tissues under a microscope.

formal_definition: prognosis is The likely outcome or course of a disease; the chance of recovery or recurrence.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's my attempt at explaining the term "prognosis" in a way that's easy to understand for a non-medical person:

Prognosis is like a movie plot twist - it's the prediction of how a disease will turn out. Imagine you're watching a thriller and you know the bad guy is going to get caught in the end, but you're not sure how it will happen. That's like a good prognosis. It means the doctor has a pretty good idea of what will happen next, like whether the patient will get better or worse. But sometimes, just like in movies, things don't always go as planned, so the prognosis can change. Just like how the bad guy might escape or the good guys might lose, a patient's condition can take an unexpected turn. So while the doctor might have a good idea of what will happen, there's always some uncertainty.</s>
easy_definition: The pattern and outcome of a disease.

formal_definition: psychosocial is In medicine, having to do with the mental, emotional, social, and spiritual effects of a disease, such as cancer. Some of the psychosocial effects of cancer are changes in how a patient thinks, their feelings, moods, beliefs, ways of coping, and relationships with family, friends, and co-workers. There are different kinds of psychosocial support that can help cancer patients, including counseling, education, group support, and spiritual support.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's my attempt to explain the term "psychosocial" in a way that's easy to understand for a non-medical person:

When someone has cancer, it affects not just their body, but also their mind and spirit. The psychosocial effects of cancer mean the mental, emotional, and social changes they go through because of their illness. It can be like when you have a big test coming up at school and it makes you feel stressed and anxious. Or when you break your arm playing sports and it's hard to do things you normally do. Cancer can change how someone feels, thinks, and acts, and it can also affect their relationships with people around them. To help cancer patients deal with these changes, there are special kinds of support called counseling, education, group support, and spiritual support. These can give them tools to cope with their cancer and feel better overall.</s>
easy_definition: Having to do with the mental, emotional, social, and spiritual aspects.

formal_definition: psychosocial support is In medicine, support given to help meet the mental, emotional, social, and spiritual needs of patients and their families. Diseases, such as cancer, can affect a patient’s thoughts, feelings, moods, beliefs, ways of coping, and relationships with family, friends, and co-workers. There are different kinds of psychosocial support that can help cancer patients. These include counseling, education, group support, and spiritual support.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's my attempt to explain "psychosocial support" in simple language for a non-medical person:

Psychosocial support is like having a team of people who care about you and want to help you feel better. When you're sick or going through a tough time, it can be hard to deal with all the emotions and changes happening inside and outside of you. Psychosocial support helps by giving you someone to talk to, teaching you new ways to cope with your feelings, and connecting you with others who understand what you're going through. It's like having a safety net of support to catch you when you need it.

For example, imagine you're playing a game of basketball and you get injured. Your coach and teammates will give you support and encouragement to help you feel better and get back in the game. That's kind of like what psychosocial support does for people dealing with illnesses or difficult situations. It provides emotional and social support to help them heal and move forward.</s>
easy_definition: Support given to help meet the mental, emotional, social, and spiritual needs. Types of psychosocial support include counseling, education, group support, and spiritual support.

formal_definition: radiation oncologist is A doctor who has special training in using radiation to treat cancer.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's an explanation of what a radiation oncologist does in simple language that a non-medical person can understand:

A radiation oncologist is a doctor who specializes in using radiation to treat cancer. Imagine your body is like a garden, and cancer is like a weed that needs to be removed. A radiation oncologist uses a very precise kind of "weeding tool" called radiation therapy to kill the cancer cells while leaving the healthy parts of the garden (your body) unharmed. They use special machines that aim tiny doses of radiation directly at the cancer, much like how a gardener might use a small watering can to target a specific weed. This helps to shrink the cancer and stop it from growing, giving you more time to live a normal life.</s>
easy_definition: A doctor who’s an expert in treating cancer with radiation.

formal_definition: radiation therapy is The use of high-energy radiation from x-rays, gamma rays, neutrons, protons, and other sources to kill cancer cells and shrink tumors. Radiation may come from a machine outside the body (external-beam radiation therapy), or it may come from radioactive material placed in the body near cancer cells (internal radiation therapy or brachytherapy). Systemic radiation therapy uses a radioactive substance, such as a radiolabeled monoclonal antibody, that travels in the blood to tissues throughout the body. Also called irradiation and radiotherapy.
simplified_explanation: Radiation therapy is like a superhero for cancer cells! It's a way to kill them off using special kinds of energy beams. Imagine your body has bad guys like cancer cells growing inside you, and these beams are like powerful weapons that can find and destroy those bad guys. There are different ways to get these beams into your body, like using machines outside of you or putting something special inside you. Either way, they work by sending the energy beams directly to the cancer cells to zapping them until they're gone! Just like how Superman would save the day by using his X-ray vision to defeat the bad guys, radiation therapy helps save lives by defeating cancer cells.</s>
easy_definition: The use of high-energy radiation from x-rays, gamma rays, neutrons, protons, and other sources to kill cancer cells and shrink tumors. Radiation may come from a machine outside the body (external-beam radiation therapy), or it may come from radioactive material placed in the body near cancer cells (internal radiation therapy or brachytherapy). Systemic radiation therapy uses a radioactive substance, such as a radiolabeled monoclonal antibody, that travels in the blood to tissues throughout the body. Also called irradiation and radiotherapy.

formal_definition: recurrence is Cancer that has recurred (come back), usually after a period of time during which the cancer could not be detected. The cancer may come back to the same place as the original (primary) tumor or to another place in the body. Also called recurrent cancer.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's an explanation of the term "recurrence" in simple language:

Recurrence means when cancer comes back after a while. Imagine you have a toy box full of blocks. If you take out one block and put it back in the box, it's still part of the same box. But if you take out a block and then later find it in a different toy box, that's like recurrence. It's like the cancer cells are moving around and showing up in new places. It can happen after a long time, like years, after the first cancer was treated. It can also come back in the same place where the first cancer was. So, recurrence is like the cancer cells coming back to haunt us again.</s>
easy_definition: Cancer that has recurred (come back), usually after a period of time during which the cancer could not be detected. The cancer may come back to the same place as the original (primary) tumor or to another place in the body. Also called recurrent cancer.

formal_definition: refractory cancer is Cancer that does not respond to treatment. The cancer may be resistant at the beginning of treatment or it may become resistant during treatment. Also called resistant cancer.
simplified_explanation: Refractory cancer means that the cancer cells don't respond well to treatment. Imagine your body is like a garden, and the cancer cells are weeds that keep growing back no matter how much water and sunlight you give them. They just won't die, even with all the medicine and therapy you try. It's like trying to kill weeds in your garden that keep coming back year after year. Same thing with cancer cells. They can be resistant to treatment from the start or become resistant over time. So, doctors might have to try different approaches to treat the cancer.</s>
easy_definition: A cancer that does not improve with treatment.

formal_definition: relapse is The return of a disease or the signs and symptoms of a disease after a period of improvement. Relapse also refers to returning to the use of an addictive substance or behavior, such as cigarette smoking.
simplified_explanation: Relapse is like a rollercoaster ride. Imagine you're on a train that's moving forward, and you've just reached the top of a hill. You feel great, you're making progress, and then... whoosh! The train starts going backward, and you find yourself back where you started. That's kind of like what happens when someone has a relapse. They were doing well, making progress, and then they start feeling sick again or using something they were trying to stop. It's like the train went off the tracks and took them back to the beginning. But don't worry, with the right help and support, people can get back on track and keep moving forward again.</s>
easy_definition: The return or worsening of cancer after a period of improvement.

formal_definition: reproductive system is The tissues, glands, and organs involved in producing offspring (children). In women, the reproductive system includes the ovaries, the fallopian tubes, the uterus, the cervix, and the vagina. In men, it includes the prostate, the testes, and the penis.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's an explanation of the reproductive system that's easy to understand and free from technical jargon:

The reproductive system is like a factory that makes babies! In women, it's like a assembly line where eggs are made and then sent through a series of conveyor belts until they reach the baby. The eggs are nurtured and grown inside the uterus, where they develop into babies. In men, it's like a production line where sperm are made and stored in special factories called testes. When a man and woman come together, their bodies work together like a well-oiled machine to create a new baby. It's a pretty amazing process, isn't it?</s>
easy_definition: The group of organs that work together to make a baby. They include the vagina, cervix, uterus, fallopian tubes, ovaries, penis, testicles, and prostate.

formal_definition: semen is The fluid that is released through the penis during orgasm. Semen is made up of sperm from the testicles and fluid from the prostate and other sex glands.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's how I would explain the term "semen" to a non-medical person:

Semen is like a special liquid that comes out of a guy's body during sexual climax. It's made up of tiny living things called sperm, which help create new life, along with some other fluids from his body like pee. When a guy gets aroused, his body releases this liquid through his penis, and it's one of the ways he can share love and pleasure with his partner. Just like how we drink milk from our mothers to get nutrition, semen helps create new life for a baby growing inside its mother. And just like how we have different flavors of milk, semen can also come in different amounts or qualities depending on various factors like health or age.</s>
easy_definition: The fluid that is released through the penis during orgasm. Semen is made up of sperm from the testicles and fluid from the prostate and other sex glands. Also called ejaculate.

formal_definition: side effect is An effect of a drug or other type of treatment that is in addition to or beyond its desired effect. Side effects can be harmful or beneficial, and most go away on their own over time. Others may last past treatment or appear long after treatment has ended. Some common side effects of cancer treatment are nausea, vomiting, fatigue, pain, decreased blood cell counts, hair loss, and mouth sores.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's my attempt to explain the term "side effect" in a way that's easy to understand for a non-medical person:

Imagine you're baking a delicious cake, and you add a special ingredient called "magic powder." The magic powder makes the cake taste amazing, but it also gives you a tiny tingle on your tongue. That tingle is like a side effect - it's something extra that happens when you take the medicine, even though it's not the main reason you took it.

Just like how the tingle on your tongue might be a good thing or a bad thing depending on what you're eating, side effects can be helpful or unhelpful. Sometimes they go away on their own, like when you stop taking the medicine. Other times, they can last longer or come back even after you stop treatment.

Cancer treatment can cause some pretty big side effects, like feeling sick to your stomach or losing your hair. These side effects can be tricky because they might happen even though you're trying to feel better from the cancer itself. But don't worry, there are ways to manage them so you can still get the help you need without feeling too uncomfortable.</s>
easy_definition: An unhealthy or unpleasant physical or emotional response to treatment.

formal_definition: sperm is The male reproductive cell, formed in the testicle. A sperm unites with an egg to form an embryo.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's how I would explain the term "sperm" to a non-medical person in a way that's easy to understand:

A sperm is like a tiny little superhero that helps create a new life! Inside a man's body, these special cells are made in his testicles. They're really small - about 1/100th of a millimeter big! When a man and woman love each other and want to have a baby, the sperm goes into the woman's body and finds an egg. Then, they come together and form a tiny baby growing inside the woman's tummy. It's a pretty amazing process!</s>
easy_definition: Cell formed in the testicle.

formal_definition: sperm aspiration is A procedure in which a sample of sperm cells is removed from the testicle or epididymis through a small needle attached to a syringe. The sperm is looked at under a microscope in the laboratory, where it may be used right away to fertilize eggs or frozen for future infertility treatment. Sperm aspiration may be useful for men who have fertility problems caused by a blockage that keeps sperm from being ejaculated. It may also be useful for men who want to have children after having treatment that may cause infertility, such as certain cancer treatments. Sperm aspiration is a type of sperm retrieval method.
simplified_explanation:
Okay! Here's how I would explain "sperm aspiration" in simple language:

Sperm aspiration is a medical procedure where doctors use a tiny needle to take a sample of sperm from a man's body. They do this so they can look at the sperm under a special microscope and see if it's healthy enough to be used to help him father a baby. This might be helpful for guys who have trouble making babies because something is blocking their sperm from coming out during ejaculation, or because they had some medical treatment that made them infertile. It's like taking a small sample of a delicious recipe book from your kitchen so you can see what ingredients you need to make your favorite dish.

In simpler terms, sperm aspiration is a way for doctors to collect a small amount of sperm from a man's body using a very thin needle. They then examine the sperm under a microscope to see if it's healthy and can be used to help the man have a baby. This procedure can be helpful for men who have trouble making babies due to a blockage in their reproductive system or because they had medical treatment that affected their fertility.</s>
easy_definition: A procedure in which a sample of sperm cells is removed from the testicle through a small needle attached to a syringe.

formal_definition: sperm banking is The process of freezing sperm and storing it for future use. Samples of semen are collected and checked under a microscope in the laboratory to count sperm cells and find out how healthy they are. The sperm cells are then frozen and stored. Sperm banking is often used for men who want to have children after having treatment that may cause infertility, such as certain cancer treatments. Sperm banking is a type of fertility preservation. Also called sperm cryopreservation.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's my attempt to explain "sperm banking" in a way that's easy to understand for a non-medical person:

Imagine your body is like a garden, and sperm are like the seeds that help plants grow. Just like how you might want to save seeds from one year to plant again next year, sperm banking is like saving some of these special seeds so they can be used to grow a baby in the future. We take a little sample of the good seeds and put them in a special freezer to keep them safe until we need them. This can be helpful for people who might need medical treatment that could make them unable to have babies later on. It's like putting aside some extra seeds just in case you want to plant a beautiful garden someday.</s>
easy_definition: The process of freezing sperm and storing it for future use. Samples of semen are collected and checked under a microscope in the laboratory to count sperm cells and find out how healthy they are. The sperm cells are then frozen and stored. Also called sperm cryopreservation.

formal_definition: sperm count is A count of the number of sperm in a sample of semen. A sperm count may be used as a measure of fertility.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's my explanation of "sperm count" for a non-medical person:

A sperm count is a way doctors measure how many sperm are in a man's semen. It's like counting the number of marbles in a jar. Just like how you need a certain number of marbles to play a game or build something, a man needs a certain number of healthy sperm to make a baby. If he has too few or too many sperm, it can make it harder for him and his partner to get pregnant. Doctors use this test to figure out if there might be a problem with the sperm or if there are other things going on that could be causing trouble getting pregnant.</s>
easy_definition: A count of the number of sperm in a sample of semen. A sperm count may be used as a measure of fertility.

formal_definition: 3D-CRT is A procedure that uses a computer to create a 3-dimensional picture of the tumor. This allows doctors to give the highest possible dose of radiation to the tumor, while sparing the normal tissue as much as possible. Also called 3-dimensional conformal radiation therapy and 3-dimensional radiation therapy.
simplified_explanation: Term '3D-CRT' not found in medical dictionary.
easy_definition: Three-dimensional conformal radiation therapy.

formal_definition: adenocarcinoma is Cancer that forms in the glandular tissue, which lines certain internal organs and makes and releases substances in the body, such as mucus, digestive juices, and other fluids. Most cancers of the breast, lung, esophagus, stomach, colon, rectum, pancreas, prostate, and uterus are adenocarcinomas.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's an explanation of the term "adenocarcinoma" in simple language:

Adenocarcinoma is a type of cancer that starts in cells that line different parts of your body, like your breasts, lungs, or digestive system. These cells are called glands, and they produce things like milk, mucus, or digestive enzymes. When these cells grow abnormally and form a tumor, it can be cancerous. Adenocarcinomas are the most common kind of cancer for many organs in your body, including the breast, lung, and digestive system. So, if you hear a doctor say someone has adenocarcinoma, it means the cancer started in the glands that line that organ.</s>
easy_definition: Cancer that forms in the glandular tissue, which lines certain internal organs and makes and releases substances in the body, such as mucus, digestive juices, and other fluids. Most cancers of the breast, lung, esophagus, stomach, colon, rectum, pancreas, prostate, and uterus are adenocarcinomas.

formal_definition: alveoli is Tiny air sacs at the end of the bronchioles (tiny branches of air tubes in the lungs). The alveoli are where the lungs and the blood exchange oxygen and carbon dioxide during the process of breathing in and breathing out. Oxygen breathed in from the air passes through the alveoli and into the blood and travels to the tissues throughout the body. Carbon dioxide travels in the blood from the body's tissues and passes through the alveoli to be breathed out.
simplified_explanation: Alright! Let me explain what "alveoli" means in simple terms. So, you know how you breathe in air through your nose or mouth and it goes down to your lungs? Well, inside those lungs, there are tiny little air sacs called alveoli. They're like tiny balloons that help your body trade oxygen for carbon dioxide. When you inhale, oxygen from the air enters these sacs and gets absorbed into your bloodstream, giving your body the oxygen it needs to work properly. And when you exhale, the carbon dioxide in your blood moves back into the alveoli and gets breathed out. It's like a little exchange center inside your lungs!</s>
easy_definition: The tiny sacs in the lungs where gases are transferred in and out of the blood.

formal_definition: bronchoscope is A thin, tube-like instrument used to examine the inside of the trachea, bronchi (air passages that lead to the lungs), and lungs. A bronchoscope has a light and a lens for viewing, and may have a tool to remove tissue.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's my attempt to explain the term "bronchoscope" in simple language:

A bronchoscope is like a tiny telescope that doctors use to look inside your airways. It's a thin, flexible tube with a light and lens on one end, and sometimes tools to remove tissue or fluids on the other end. Imagine holding a paperclip-sized tube up to your mouth and nose, and suddenly you can see inside your lungs! Doctors use bronchoscopes to check if everything is working properly in there, like if there are any problems with your breathing or if there are any bad guys (like cancer cells) hiding inside. It's a pretty cool tool that helps doctors keep you healthy!</s>
easy_definition: A device that is guided down the throat to look inside the airways.

formal_definition: bronchoscopy is A procedure that uses a bronchoscope to examine the inside of the trachea, bronchi (air passages that lead to the lungs), and lungs. A bronchoscope is a thin, tube-like instrument with a light and a lens for viewing. It may also have a tool to remove tissue to be checked under a microscope for signs of disease. The bronchoscope is inserted through the nose or mouth. Bronchoscopy may be used to detect cancer or to perform some treatment procedures.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's how I would explain the term "bronchoscopy" to a non-medical person:

Bronchoscopy is a test where a doctor puts a special tool called a bronchoscope inside your body through your nose or mouth. The bronchoscope has a light and a lens that lets the doctor see inside your airways, like a tunnel that leads to your lungs. The doctor might use the bronchoscope to look for any problems, like cancer, or to do some treatments, like removing some bad cells. It's kind of like using a flashlight to check out a dark tunnel, but instead of a flashlight, it's a special tool that helps the doctor see inside your body.</s>
easy_definition: A procedure to look inside the airways with a device that is guided down the throat.

formal_definition: bronchus is A large airway that leads from the trachea (windpipe) to a lung. The plural of bronchus is bronchi.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's how I would explain the term "bronchus" to a non-medical person:

The bronchus is like a big pipe that connects your throat to one of your lungs. It's like a long tunnel that air goes through to get to your lung, so your lung can take in oxygen and breathe properly. There are actually two bronchi, one for each lung, and they're kind of like two separate pipes running parallel to each other. So, when you breathe in, the air goes through these bronchi and into your lungs, where it can pick up oxygen and help keep your body working properly.</s>
easy_definition: One of the two main airways that extends into the lungs.

formal_definition: carcinoma is Cancer that begins in the skin or in tissues that line or cover internal organs.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's my explanation of "carcinoma" in simple language:

Carcinoma is a type of cancer that starts in the skin or the lining of internal organs. Imagine your skin as a canvas, and cancer cells are like paint splattering on it. Just like how paint can stain and damage the canvas, cancer cells can grow and multiply, causing problems for your body. Carcinomas can form in different parts of the body, such as the breast, lung, or colon. They can also spread to other areas, like a weed growing in a garden, and can cause serious health issues if left untreated. It's important to catch carcinomas early on so they don't have time to spread and become harder to treat.</s>
easy_definition: A cancer of cells that line the inner or outer surfaces of the body.

formal_definition: chemoimmunotherapy is Chemotherapy combined with immunotherapy. Chemotherapy uses different drugs to kill or slow the growth of cancer cells; immunotherapy uses treatments to stimulate or restore the ability of the immune system to fight cancer.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's an explanation of chemoimmunotherapy that's easy to understand and relatable:

Chemoimmunotherapy is like a superhero team up! It's when doctors use two different types of medicine together to fight cancer. One set of medicine, called chemotherapy, works by directly killing or slowing down the growth of cancer cells. The other set of medicine, called immunotherapy, helps your body's own army of disease-fighters (called the immune system) recognize and attack cancer cells more effectively. Together, these two teams of medicine give your body a powerful boost to beat back cancer. Just like how Iron Man and Captain America work together to save the world!</s>
easy_definition: A combined treatment with both chemotherapy and immunotherapy.

formal_definition: chemoradiation is Treatment that combines chemotherapy with radiation therapy. Also called chemoradiotherapy.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's an explanation of "chemoradiation" in simple language that's easy to understand:

Chemoradiation is when doctors use both chemotherapy and radiation therapy together to treat cancer. It's like using two different weapons to fight the cancer battle. Chemotherapy is like throwing a magic potion that kills cancer cells, while radiation therapy is like aiming a special kind of light beam at the cancer cells to destroy them. Together, they can be even more effective at killing cancer cells than either one alone. The doctors will carefully plan how much of each treatment to give you, based on the type of cancer you have and where it is in your body. Just like how you need the right amount of medicine for a fever, chemoradiation needs to be just the right amount for your cancer.</s>
easy_definition: A cancer treatment with both cell-killing drugs and high-energy rays.

formal_definition: chemotherapy is Treatment that uses drugs to stop the growth of cancer cells, either by killing the cells or by stopping them from dividing. Chemotherapy may be given by mouth, injection, or infusion, or on the skin, depending on the type and stage of the cancer being treated. It may be given alone or with other treatments, such as surgery, radiation therapy, or biologic therapy.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's how I would explain the term "chemotherapy" in simple language for a non-medical person:

Chemotherapy is a kind of medicine that doctors use to treat cancer. It works by stopping or killing the cancer cells inside your body. Imagine your body is like a garden, and the cancer cells are like weeds that need to be removed. Chemotherapy is like a special tool that helps get rid of those weeds so your body can stay healthy again. Doctors can give you chemotherapy through a vein in your arm, on your skin, or even by swallowing a pill. It might be given alone or with other treatments, like surgery or radiation therapy. The goal is to help your body fight off the cancer and feel better.</s>
easy_definition: Treatment that uses drugs to stop the growth of cancer cells, either by killing the cells or by stopping them from dividing. Chemotherapy may be given by mouth, injection, or infusion, or on the skin, depending on the type and stage of the cancer being treated. It may be given alone or with other treatments, such as surgery, radiation therapy, or biologic therapy.

formal_definition: chest wall is The skin, fat, muscles, bones, and other tissues that form a protective structure around vital organs in the area between the neck and the abdomen, including the heart, major blood vessels, lungs, and liver. The bones in the chest wall include the ribs, sternum (breastbone), and spine. The chest wall also helps support breathing and movement of the upper arms and shoulders.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's how I would explain the term "chest wall" to a non-medical person:

So, have you ever thought about your chest? You know, the part where your heart is? Well, that's actually made up of different parts like skin, muscles, and bones all working together to keep everything safe inside. It's kind of like a big hug for your insides! The chest wall helps your body breathe and move, and it even helps your arms and shoulders do their thing. And did you know that there are specific bones in there too, like the ribs, breastbone, and spine? They're like little pillars that hold everything together. Pretty cool, right?</s>
easy_definition: The layer of muscle, bone, and fat that protects the vital organs.

formal_definition: clinical stage is The stage of cancer (amount or spread of cancer in the body) that is based on tests that are done before surgery. These include physical exams, imaging tests, laboratory tests (such as blood tests), and biopsies.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's an explanation of the term "clinical stage" in simple language:

Imagine your body is like a garden with different plants growing inside of you. Cancer is like a weed that grows and spreads quickly, and doctors need to know how far it has grown to decide the best way to treat it. Clinical stage is like a map that shows where the weeds are located and how big they are. Before surgery, doctors will use different tools like X-rays, blood tests, and biopsies to create this map and find out the stage of the cancer. The stage helps doctors figure out the best way to remove the cancer cells and give you the best chance of getting better.

For example, if the cancer is only in one spot, it might be considered stage 1. If it has spread to nearby areas, it could be stage 2. And if it has spread even further, it could be stage 3 or 4. Knowing the stage of the cancer helps doctors come up with a plan to attack the cancer and give you the best chance of beating it.</s>
easy_definition: The rating of the extent of cancer before treatment is started.

formal_definition: diagnosis is The process of identifying a disease, condition, or injury from its signs and symptoms. A health history, physical exam, and tests, such as blood tests, imaging tests, and biopsies, may be used to help make a diagnosis.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to explain the term "diagnosis" in a way that's easy to understand!

Diagnosis is like solving a mystery. When someone gets sick or hurt, we try to figure out what's wrong with them by looking at their symptoms and using different tools like medical tests. It's like piecing together a puzzle to find the right solution. We use all the clues we can find to make an educated guess about what's causing the problem, just like how you might try to solve a mystery book by following the clues in the story. And just like how solving a mystery can help you figure out what happened, making a diagnosis helps us figure out what's wrong with someone so we can give them the right treatment.</s>
easy_definition: An identification of an illness based on tests.

formal_definition: esophagus is The muscular tube through which food passes from the throat to the stomach.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's my explanation of the term "esophagus" for a non-medical person:

The esophagus is like a long, narrow tube that connects your mouth to your stomach. It's like a little tunnel that food goes through so it can get to your belly where it can be broken down and digested. Imagine if you had a hose that you could swallow food through, and it would go straight into your belly without having to chew or taste it first - that's kind of like what the esophagus does! It helps move food around your body so it can be properly digested and absorbed by your body.</s>
easy_definition: The tube-shaped organ between the mouth and stomach.

formal_definition: FDA is An agency in the U.S. federal government whose mission is to protect public health by making sure that food, cosmetics, and nutritional supplements are safe to use and truthfully labeled. The FDA also makes sure that drugs, medical devices, and equipment are safe and effective, and that blood for transfusions and transplant tissue are safe. Also called Food and Drug Administration.
simplified_explanation: Term 'FDA' not found in medical dictionary.
easy_definition: Food and Drug Administration.

formal_definition: immunotherapy is A type of therapy that uses substances to stimulate or suppress the immune system to help the body fight cancer, infection, and other diseases. Some types of immunotherapy only target certain cells of the immune system. Others affect the immune system in a general way. Types of immunotherapy include cytokines, vaccines, bacillus Calmette-Guerin (BCG), and some monoclonal antibodies.
simplified_explanation: Immunotherapy is like a superhero for your immune system! It's a special kind of treatment that helps your body fight off bad guys like cancer cells or germs. Just like how Superman has powers to save the day, immunotherapy gives your immune system extra strength to battle against sicknesses. There are different kinds of immunotherapy, like cytokines, vaccines, and monoclonal antibodies, each with their own unique abilities. For example, cytokines are like messengers that tell your immune system what to do, while vaccines teach your body how to recognize and defeat bad guys on its own. BCG is like a special suit that protects your immune system from harmful germs. With immunotherapy, your body becomes stronger and better able to defend itself against disease!</s>
easy_definition: A type of therapy that uses substances to stimulate or suppress the immune system to help the body fight cancer, infection, and other diseases. Some types of immunotherapy only target certain cells of the immune system. Others affect the immune system in a general way. Types of immunotherapy include cytokines, vaccines, bacillus Calmette-Guerin (BCG), and some monoclonal antibodies.

formal_definition: lobe is A portion of an organ, such as the liver, lung, breast, thyroid, or brain.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's how I would explain the term "lobe" in a way that's easy to understand for a non-medical person:

A lobe is like a separate part of an organ. Imagine your liver is like a big football. The liver has different parts, called lobes, that are like different sections of the football field. Each lobe has its own job to do to keep your body healthy. For example, one lobe might be responsible for filtering out bad stuff from your blood, while another lobe helps regulate your metabolism. Similarly, the brain has lobes too - the frontal lobe helps us think and make decisions, while the temporal lobe processes sounds and memories. So, just like how each section of a football field has a specific role, each lobe of an organ has its own important function.</s>
easy_definition: A clearly seen division in an organ.

formal_definition: lobectomy is Surgery to remove a whole lobe (section) of an organ (such as the lungs, liver, brain, or thyroid gland).

Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's my explanation of a lobectomy in simple language:

A lobectomy is a surgery where a doctor removes a whole section of an organ, like a lung or liver. For example, if someone has cancer in their lung, the doctor might do a lobectomy to take out the part of the lung where the cancer is growing. This helps stop the cancer from spreading and makes it easier to treat.

I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any other questions.</s>
easy_definition: An operation that removes a whole lobe of an organ.

formal_definition: lymph node is A small bean-shaped structure that is part of the body’s immune system. Lymph nodes filter substances that travel through the lymphatic fluid, and they contain lymphocytes (white blood cells) that help the body fight infection and disease. There are hundreds of lymph nodes found throughout the body. They are connected to one another by lymph vessels. Clusters of lymph nodes are found in the neck, axilla (underarm), chest, abdomen, and groin. For example, there are about 20-40 lymph nodes in the axilla. Also called lymph gland.
simplified_explanation: Lymph nodes are like tiny superheroes that protect our bodies from germs and diseases! They're small, bean-shaped structures found all over our body, like in the neck, armpits, stomach, and groin. Like a police station for our immune system, lymph nodes filter out bad guys like viruses and bacteria that try to harm us. Inside these nodes, special white blood cells called lymphocytes work around the clock to keep us healthy. There are hundreds of them scattered throughout our body, all connected by tiny channels called lymph vessels. So next time you hear someone say "I have a fever," remember, your body's lymph nodes are working hard to keep you safe!</s>
easy_definition: A small bean-shaped structure that is part of the body’s immune system. Lymph nodes filter substances that travel through the lymphatic fluid, and they contain lymphocytes (white blood cells) that help the body fight infection and disease. There are hundreds of lymph nodes found throughout the body. They are connected to one another by lymph vessels. Clusters of lymph nodes are found in the neck, axilla (underarm), chest, abdomen, and groin. For example, there are about 20-40 lymph nodes in the axilla. Also called lymph gland.

formal_definition: mediastinoscopy is A procedure in which a mediastinoscope is used to examine the organs in the area between the lungs and nearby lymph nodes. A mediastinoscope is a thin, tube-like instrument with a light and a lens for viewing. It may also have a tool to remove tissue to be checked under a microscope for signs of disease. The mediastinoscope is inserted into the chest through an incision above the breastbone. This procedure is usually done to get a tissue sample from the lymph nodes on the right side of the chest.

Okay, I'm here to help! Here's my explanation of mediastinoscopy:

Imagine you have a big box of toys inside your body called your mediastinum. This box contains important organs like the heart, trachea, and lungs. Sometimes doctors need to take a closer look at these organs to see if there might be something wrong with them. So, they use a special tool called a mediastinoscope to peek inside the box without having to open it up completely. They insert the scope through a small cut above your breastbone, like putting in a straw through a hole in a cardboard box. Then, they can see inside the box and take out any bad toys (tissue samples) to check under a microscope for diseases. It's like taking out the trash from a messy room!</s>

formal_definition: mediastinum is The area between the lungs. The organs in this area include the heart and its large blood vessels, the trachea, the esophagus, the thymus, and lymph nodes but not the lungs.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's an explanation of the term "mediastinum" that's easy to understand:

The mediastinum is like a big playground in your body. It's the space between your two lungs, and it's where some of your most important organs live. Inside the mediastinum, you'll find the heart and its big blood vessels, the trachea (which takes air from your nose or mouth to your lungs), the esophagus (which carries food from your throat to your stomach), and some tiny organs called lymph nodes that help keep you healthy. All these organs work together to keep your body running smoothly. So, just like how you need a big playground with different activities for kids to have fun, your body needs its own mediastinum to keep all its important organs happy and healthy!</s>
easy_definition: The area of the chest between the lungs.

formal_definition: medical history is A record of information about a person’s health. A personal medical history may include information about allergies, illnesses, surgeries, immunizations, and results of physical exams and tests. It may also include information about medicines taken and health habits, such as diet and exercise. A family medical history includes health information about a person’s close family members (parents, grandparents, children, brothers, and sisters). This includes their current and past illnesses. A family medical history may show a pattern of certain diseases in a family. Also called health history.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's how I would explain "medical history" to a non-medical person:

Your medical history is like a book that keeps track of your body's past experiences. It includes information about any sicknesses or injuries you've had, any medicine you've taken, and how well you take care of yourself. Just like how you might keep a journal of special events in your life, your medical history keeps track of special things that happen to your body. Your doctor uses this information to help them figure out what might be wrong if you get sick or hurt in the future. And, just like how you might ask your parents about their experiences when you were little, your doctor might ask you about your parents' and other close relatives' health problems too. All of this information helps doctors understand your body better and give you the best possible care.</s>
easy_definition: A report of all your health events and medications.

formal_definition: metastasis is The spread of cancer cells from the place where they first formed to another part of the body. In metastasis, cancer cells break away from the original (primary) tumor, travel through the blood or lymph system, and form a new tumor in other organs or tissues of the body. The new, metastatic tumor is the same type of cancer as the primary tumor. For example, if breast cancer spreads to the lung, the cancer cells in the lung are breast cancer cells, not lung cancer cells.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's my attempt to explain the term "metastasis" in simple language:

Metastasis is like a game of tag. Cancer cells are like players, and they can move from one organ to another, just like how kids play tag. When the cancer cells go to a new place, they start a new game, forming a new tumor. This new tumor is made up of the same type of cancer cells that were in the original tumor. So, even though the cancer cells are now in a different place, they still act like the same team. For example, if breast cancer cells escape from the breast and go to the lungs, they will still be breast cancer cells, not lung cancer cells. It's like the cancer cells are trying to catch each other and keep playing the game of tag, but they never stop moving and spreading to new places.</s>
easy_definition: The spread of cancer cells from the place where they first formed to another part of the body. In metastasis, cancer cells break away from the original (primary) tumor, travel through the blood or lymph system, and form a new tumor in other organs or tissues of the body. The new, metastatic tumor is the same type of cancer as the primary tumor. For example, if breast cancer spreads to the lung, the cancer cells in the lung are breast cancer cells, not lung cancer cells.

formal_definition: neoadjuvant therapy is Treatment given as a first step to shrink a tumor before the main treatment, which is usually surgery, is given. Examples of neoadjuvant therapy include chemotherapy, radiation therapy, and hormone therapy. It is a type of induction therapy.
simplified_explanation: Neoadjuvant therapy is like a warm-up exercise for your body's cancer fight. It's given before the main treatment, like surgery, to help shrink the tumor down so it's easier to remove. Think of it like a football team doing drills before a game to get ready for their opponents. The therapy can be chemotherapy, radiation, or hormones that target the cancer cells and weaken them before the main attack. This way, when the surgery comes, the tumor is smaller and easier to remove, giving the patient a better chance of recovery.</s>
easy_definition: Treatment given as a first step to shrink a tumor before the main treatment, which is usually surgery, is given. Examples of neoadjuvant therapy include chemotherapy, radiation therapy, and hormone therapy. It is a type of induction therapy.

formal_definition: nodule is A growth or lump that may be malignant (cancer) or benign (not cancer).
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's my attempt at explaining the term "nodule" in a way that's easy to understand:

A nodule is like a small bump on your body that could be either good or bad news. It could be a sign of something serious, like cancer, or it could be nothing to worry about. Imagine finding a small marble on your skin - that's kind of like a nodule. But instead of being a fun toy, it could be a sign of something inside your body that needs attention from a doctor. So, if you notice a nodule on your body, it's important to see a doctor right away to find out what it is and figure out the best course of action.</s>
easy_definition: A small mass of tissue.

formal_definition: pneumonectomy is Surgery to remove all of one lung. In a partial pneumonectomy, one or more lobes of a lung are removed.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's my explanation of pneumonectomy in simple language:

A pneumonectomy is a surgery where a doctor removes all of one lung. This means that they take out the entire lung, leaving the other lung intact. In some cases, doctors might only remove part of a lung, called a partial pneumonectomy. Think of it like cutting off a piece of bread - if you cut off a small piece, it's called a partial slice, but if you cut off the whole loaf, it's called a full pneumonectomy. Just like how you need both lungs to breathe properly, your body needs both lobes of your lung to work right. So, when a doctor takes out one or both lobes, it can affect how well your body breathes.</s>
easy_definition: An operation that removes the entire lung.

formal_definition: primary tumor is A term used to describe the original, or first, tumor in the body. Cancer cells from a primary tumor may spread to other parts of the body and form new, or secondary, tumors. This is called metastasis. These secondary tumors are the same type of cancer as the primary tumor. Also called primary cancer.
simplified_explanation:
Okay, I'd be happy to help! Here's an explanation of the term "primary tumor" that's easy to understand:

Think of your body like a house. The primary tumor is like a big ol' messy room inside that house. It's where the cancer cells start growing and multiplying. Just like how a room can get cluttered and out of control, a primary tumor can grow and spread to other parts of the body. When this happens, it's called metastasis. So, just like how you might clean up one room before moving on to another, doctors try to remove the primary tumor to stop the cancer from spreading. Makes sense?</s>
easy_definition: The main mass of a certain type of cancer cell.

formal_definition: pulmonologist is A doctor who has special training in diagnosing and treating diseases of the lungs. Also called pulmonary specialist.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's an explanation of the term "pulmonologist" that's easy to understand:

A pulmonologist is a type of doctor who specializes in taking care of people with problems related to their lungs. Think of your lungs like two sponges that filter the air you breathe in and out. If one of those sponges gets dirty or punctured, you might need to see a pulmonologist to get it cleaned up and working properly again. These doctors have extra training to fix things like coughs, colds, pneumonia, and other breathing issues. They can also help you manage conditions like asthma or chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). So, if you have trouble breathing or feel like something is wrong with your lungs, see a pulmonologist for help!</s>
easy_definition: A doctor who’s an expert in lung diseases.

formal_definition: respiratory system is The organs that are involved in breathing. These include the nose, throat, larynx, trachea, bronchi, and lungs. Also called respiratory tract.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's my attempt to explain the term "respiratory system" in simple language for a non-medical person:

So, have you ever breathed in or out? That's right - the respiratory system is like a magic machine inside your body that helps you do just that! It's made up of different parts like the nose, throat, voice box, windpipe, and lungs. When you breathe in, these parts work together to take in air from the outside world and bring it into your body. And when you breathe out, they help get rid of the bad stuff you don't need anymore. Pretty cool, huh?</s>
easy_definition: The group of organs that transfers gases in and out of the body.

formal_definition: segmentectomy is Surgery to remove part of an organ or gland. It may also be used to remove a tumor and normal tissue around it. In lung cancer surgery, segmentectomy refers to removing a section of a lobe of the lung. Also called segmental resection.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's my attempt at explaining the term "segmentectomy" in simple language:

A segmentectomy is a type of surgery where doctors remove a small piece of an organ or gland, like a slice of pizza from a pie. They might take out just the part that has a tumor or abnormal cells, leaving the rest of the organ healthy. This is different from a lobectomy, which is when they remove a whole lobe of the organ, like taking the entire slice of pizza. Segmentectomy is often used for lung cancer surgery, where they remove a section of a lobe of the lung to get rid of the bad cells. Just like how you might take a bite out of a pizza to eat it, doctors take out the bad part of the organ during a segmentectomy.</s>
easy_definition: An operation that removes a large part of a lobe.

formal_definition: sleeve lobectomy is Surgery to remove a lung tumor in a lobe of the lung and a part of the main bronchus (airway). The ends of the bronchus are rejoined and any remaining lobes are reattached to the bronchus. This surgery is done to save part of the lung. Also called sleeve resection.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's an explanation of sleeve lobectomy that's easy to understand:

Imagine your lungs are like a big blanket that covers your chest. A tumor is like a small patch of fabric that's grown where it shouldn't be. During a sleeve lobectomy, the doctor will take out the bad patch of fabric (the tumor) and some of the surrounding cloth (part of the lobe and bronchus). They'll then sew the rest of the cloth back together so that it's still functional. It's kind of like how you might repair a ripped sweater by sewing two sides together again - the goal is to keep the sweater whole and useful. This surgery helps save as much of the good lung tissue as possible while getting rid of the bad stuff.</s>
easy_definition: An operation to remove an entire lobe and part of the bronchus.

formal_definition: small cell lung cancer is An aggressive (fast-growing) cancer that forms in tissues of the lung and can spread to other parts of the body. The cancer cells look small and oval-shaped when looked at under a microscope.
simplified_explanation:
Okay! Here's my attempt at explaining "small cell lung cancer" in simple language for a non-medical person:

Imagine your cells are like little soldiers in your body, working together to keep you healthy. But sometimes, some of these soldiers can turn into bad guys and start growing out of control. They can divide and multiply quickly, forming a tumor that can spread to other parts of your body. This is what happens with small cell lung cancer. It's called "small cell" because the cancer cells are really tiny, almost like pea-sized, and they grow and move fast. They can travel through your bloodstream or lymph system to other parts of your body, like your liver, bones, or brain. That's why it's important to catch this type of cancer early on, so we can treat it before it spreads too far.</s>
easy_definition: An aggressive (fast-growing) cancer that forms in tissues of the lung and can spread to other parts of the body. The cancer cells look small and oval-shaped when looked at under a microscope.

formal_definition: squamous cell carcinoma is Cancer that begins in squamous cells. Squamous cells are thin, flat cells that look like fish scales, and are found in the tissue that forms the surface of the skin, the lining of the hollow organs of the body, and the lining of the respiratory and digestive tracts. Most cancers of the anus, cervix, head and neck, and vagina are squamous cell carcinomas. Also called epidermoid carcinoma.
simplified_explanation:
Sure! Here's my attempt at explaining squamous cell carcinoma in a way that's easy to understand for a non-medical person:

Imagine your skin as a big piece of paper with tiny, flat cells on its surface. These cells are called squamous cells because they look like fish scales. Just like how your skin protects your body from outside harm, these cells help keep your insides safe too - like the lining of your hollow organs or the inside of your throat. Sometimes, bad things can happen to these cells and they can turn into cancer, which is called squamous cell carcinoma. This type of cancer can grow in different parts of your body like your anus, cervix, head, or vagina. It's important to get checked by a doctor if you notice any changes in your body so we can catch it early and treat it before it gets worse.</s>
easy_definition: A type of cancer of thin and flat cells that line the surface of organs.

formal_definition: supportive care is Care given to improve the quality of life of people who have an illness or disease by preventing or treating, as early as possible, the symptoms of the disease and the side effects caused by treatment of the disease. Supportive care includes physical, psychological, social, and spiritual support for patients and their families. There are many types of supportive care. Examples include pain management, nutritional support, counseling, exercise, music therapy, meditation, and palliative care. Supportive care may be given with other treatments from the time of diagnosis until the end of life.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's my attempt at explaining "supportive care" in simple language:

Supportive care is like having a team of superheroes working together to help you feel better when you're sick. They don't just fight the bad guys (the disease), they also look after your body, mind, and spirit to make sure you're comfortable and able to live your life as well as possible. They might give you medicine to ease your pain, teach you how to eat healthy foods to give you energy, talk to you about your feelings, help you stay active, or even play soothing music to calm you down. Just like how Superman has his Fortress of Solitude to rest and recharge, supportive care is there to help you find peace and strength during difficult times.</s>
easy_definition: Care given to improve the quality of life of people who have an illness or disease by preventing or treating, as early as possible, the symptoms of the disease and the side effects caused by treatment of the disease. Supportive care includes physical, psychological, social, and spiritual support for patients and their families. There are many types of supportive care. Examples include pain management, nutritional support, counseling, exercise, music therapy, meditation, and palliative care. Supportive care may be given with other treatments from the time of diagnosis until the end of life.

formal_definition: surgery is A procedure to remove or repair a part of the body or to find out whether disease is present. Also called operation.
simplified_explanation:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here's my explanation of the term "surgery" for a non-medical person:

Surgery is like a repairman fixing something that's broken. Imagine your body is like a house, and there's something inside you that needs fixing, like a leaky pipe or a broken window. A surgeon is like a skilled worker who can fix the problem by operating on you. They use special tools and techniques to make sure everything goes smoothly and safely. Just like how a repairman might need to take things apart to fix something, a surgeon might need to cut through skin and other tissues to get to what needs to be fixed. But don't worry, they'll make sure to put everything back together properly so you can feel better!</s>
easy_definition: An operation to remove or repair a part of the body.

formal_definition: targeted therapy is A type of treatment that uses drugs or other substances to target specific molecules that cancer cells need to survive and spread. Targeted therapies work in different ways to treat cancer. Some stop cancer cells from growing by interrupting signals that cause them to grow and divide, stopping signals that help form blood vessels, delivering cell-killing substances to cancer cells, or starving cancer cells of hormones they need to grow. Other targeted therapies help the immune system kill cancer cells or directly cause cancer cell death. Most targeted therapies are either small-molecule drugs or monoclonal antibodies. Also called molecularly targeted therapy.
simplified_explanation:
Okay! Here's my attempt at explaining the term "targeted therapy" in simple language for a non-medical person:

Targeted therapy is like a superhero cape for your doctor. Instead of just attacking all the bad cells in your body, like a regular cure might do, targeted therapy zeros in on the specific cells that are causing trouble. It's like having a magic spell that only works on the really bad guys, leaving the good cells alone.
